title: CMHC Insured Loans Explained: Lower Rates, Higher Leverage

blocks.text: How insured multifamily programs work, who qualifies, and why preparing early is the difference-maker. CMHC insurance lets lenders advance more, at better rates, because the federal insurer carries the default risk — but the programs reward borrowers who plan for them from day one.
What the insurance actually buys you

items: Leverage up to 95% on qualifying affordable projects
Amortizations up to 50 years under select programs
Rates priced off CMB yields — typically well inside conventional spreads
Certainty of execution once the certificate of insurance is issued

blocks.text: The premium structure, affordability commitments, and documentation standards are set before your application is submitted — and retrofitting a deal to fit a program after the fact costs both time and leverage. Energy targets, accessibility scoring, and rent commitments all earn premium discounts when they're designed in, not bolted on.
We structure CMHC applications from the first conversation, so the program works as hard for your deal as the building does.
